record.field: Umami is a simple, fast, privacy-focused alternative to Google Analytics.

services: umami: image: ghcr.io/umami-software/umami:3.0.3 restart: always healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - curl http://localhost:3000/api/heartbeat interval: 5s timeout: 5s retries: 5 depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy environment: DATABASE_URL: postgresql://umami:umami@db:5432/umami DATABASE_TYPE: postgresql APP_SECRET: {{ app_secret }} container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network db: image: postgres:15-alpine restart: always healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U $${POSTGRES_USER} -d $${POSTGRES_DB} interval: 5s timeout: 5s retries: 5 volumes: - ./db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data environment: POSTGRES_DB: umami POSTGRES_USER: umami POSTGRES_PASSWORD: umami container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
